full_text:
Received a small piece of tumor tissue from the pathology. Based on the ice-section done by pathologist Anni Virtanen, the sample was necrotic and may not have contained many vital cells. Therefore, the low viability after dissociation was expected.

The sample was minced into small pieces and dissociated using Dispase. Dispase dissociation media included 9.7 ml DMEM/F12+++ (DMEM/F12 supplemented with 1X glutamax, 10 mM HEPES, and 1% pen/strep) + 200 µl Dispase + 100 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37°C, rotating for 30 min, vortexed before and after. Filtered through a 70 µm cell strainer.

RBC lysis with 3 ml for 5 min + 15 ml DMEM/F12+++. The final pellet was resuspended in 2 mL of DMEM/F12+++ media, and for counting, a 1:10 dilution was made.

Cell counts:

Biobanking

Dispase dissociated cells: 5x 15 E6 cells

Cells were frozen in 1mL of STEM-CellBanker.

| | Dispase
1) | total [E6 cells/ml] | 11.5
 | live [E6 cells/ml] | 2.60
 | viability [%] | 22.7
 | size [µm] | 6.5
2) | total [E6 cells/ml] | 11.3
 | live [E6 cells/ml] | 2.35
 | viability [%] | 20.8
 | size [µm] | 6.4
Average | [cells/ml] | 11.4 E6 x10
= 114 E6
 | total [cells] | 228 E6 in 2 mL
 | viability [%] | 21.75
